key_points: Moody Towers described as long overdue for demolition by locals
Living on campus means sharing dorms with 4+ people
Parking is expensive, predatory ticketing up to $500-$1000
key_quotes: Moody Towers should have been all torn down 25 years ago!!!
16k.. lmao, just rent at this point with roommates nearby
her only concern is me sharing a dorm with four other people

key_points: Commuter-heavy culture makes organic friend-making genuinely hard
Active campus wildlife, cosplay cons, raccoons become social events
Drink spiking reported at tailgates; stay alert at events
key_quotes: I didn't make a single friend at UH. Even when I lived closer and tried to go on campus hoping to meet people, it just never worked out.
Don't be hard on yourself. UH isn't set up to be like this.
Please be safe and watch all your drinks like a hawk

key_points: McDonald's closed; Pizza Hut replacement won't open until Fall 2026
Nook Cafe closed with no social media announcement during grad season
Chick-fil-A in student center remains the reliable go-to option

bro WHY PIZZA HUT OF ALL PLACES
a full year bro??? my ass want it NOW

key_points: Student death on campus created visible community grief and fear
Many students feel isolated due to commuter lifestyle and online classes
Genuine peer support exists on Reddit; students look out for each other

I didn't make a single friend at UH
I love UH and college in general... everyone is doing their own fucking thing and everyone just chilling

key_points: Out-of-state tuition hits $50k/year with poor phone support access
Parking tickets can snowball to $500-$1000 with minimal grace
Strong scholarship programs exist including National Merit and full-ride options
key_quotes: I do not live in texas and will not arrive on campus until AFTER tuition is due... I am paying 50k a year
500 to 1000 if late by 2 weeks? wtf that's predatory
They did this shit to my friend... it racked up to almost 8K cuz it kept doubling and when he wanted to transfer they wouldn't let him so he just dropped out

key_points: Beloved professors exist but CS department quality gets criticized sharply
Canvas crashed during active finals; proctors scrambled with no solution
CougarGrades tool and RateMyProfessor essential for class planning
key_quotes: I hate the computer science department there. Most of the professors are absolute garbage and I honestly did not learn much
Canvas went down while I was taking a final. Images for questions wouldn't load
Dr. Bott is the only professor I knew to walk into the auditorium with a piece of chalk and nothing else. He was one of my favorite professors

key_points: Bureaucratic systems actively trap students near graduation with circular rules
2.5-hour phone hold times reported for basic out-of-state student services
Departments don't communicate; students get bounced between offices
key_quotes: I can't enroll in the summer class. My enrollment period is tomorrow and I can't enroll because I've applied to graduate.
Classic UH. None of their systems, building, departments communicate.
Welcome to UH... It does not get better once you are on campus. UH is still very disorganized

key_points: Massive ongoing construction destroying campus landmarks mid-enrollment
Cullen Fountain demolished; won't be replaced until 2027 at earliest
Off-campus area described as genuinely dangerous, especially at night
key_quotes: I fucking hate the fact that we're paying the same tuition... but our campus is infinitely more ghetto
Glad they murked this area just in time for me to graduate
Off campus is very dangerous, especially this area, please please please do not walk at night

Houston location provides strong energy/chemical industry networking
Bauer business school has decent regional employer presence
key_quotes: CS for entry level is in the fucking toilet
I'm glad I got to know him, he's one of the primary reasons I ended up in the chemical industry
Hats funny my friend went there and didn't have a high gpa but has had some great jobs

key_points: In-state tuition is reasonable; out-of-state at $50k is hard to justify
Construction chaos means paying full price for a degraded experience
Strong scholarship programs can make UH genuinely affordable for top students
key_quotes: I hope it's worth the cost cause UH definitely not worth 50k

Football culture building momentum; game atmosphere still inconsistent
